title: 10 best herbal creams for fungal skin infection

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Tea tree oil's antimicrobial properties help combat fungi, bacteria, and viruses that cause skin infections, inhibiting their growth and eliminating them from the affected area.
Antifungal activity: The antifungal compounds in tea tree oil, such as terpinen-4-ol, directly target fungal cells, preventing them from multiplying and causing infection.

Anti-inflammatory activity: Tea tree oil's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with skin infections, making it more comfortable to treat and manage the condition.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree, creams helps with fungal skin infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compound, terpinen-4-ol, has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi such as Candida and dermatophytes. This leads to a reduction in fungal load, alleviating symptoms like itching, redness, and inflammation. The cream's ability to penetrate deep into the skin allows for effective treatment of fungal infections, promoting a faster recovery and healthier skin. It's a natural and effective solution for fungal skin infections.
remedy_constituents: Terpinen-4-ol: It helps combat fungal skin infections by disrupting the cell membrane of fungal organisms, ultimately leading to their death.
Cineole (Eucalyptol): This constituent exhibits antifungal properties by inhibiting the growth of fungal cells and reducing the production of fungal toxins.
α-Terpineol: It has been shown to exhibit antifungal activity by damaging the cell membrane of fungal organisms and preventing them from reproducing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in essential oils, particularly tea tree oil, which has antimicrobial properties that help combat fungal infections.
Stems: Similar to leaves, stems also contain tea tree oil and other beneficial compounds that aid in fighting fungal infections.
Buds: Buds are another source of tea tree oil, which is used to create creams that help soothe and heal fungal skin infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 pounds of fresh melaleuca alternifolia leaves from mature plants in early morning.
Dry the leaves in a single layer at 100°f to 150°f for 24 to 48 hours.
Infuse 1 cup of dried leaves in 2 cups of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil at 100°f to 150°f for 2 to 4 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Mix 1 part of the infused oil with 1 part of beeswax and 1 part of distilled water in a double boiler to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Antifungal compounds: Herbal olive creams contain antifungal compounds such as oleuropein and hydroxytyrosol, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal species like Candida albicans, preventing infection and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The antioxidants and polyphenols present in herbal olive creams help reduce inflammation and soothe skin irritation caused by fungal infections, creating a favorable environment for wound healing.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant-rich composition of herbal olive creams helps protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to fungal infection and slow down the healing process.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Olea europaea, also known as olive, creams helps with fungal skin infection because of its antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The cream's active compounds, such as oleuropein, possess a potent ability to inhibit the growth of fungi and other microorganisms that cause skin infections. This makes Olea europaea creams an effective remedy for treating conditions like athlete's foot, ringworm, and other fungal skin infections, promoting healthy skin and reducing inflammation. Its natural and non-irritating properties also make it a popular choice for sensitive skin.
remedy_constituents: Oleuropein: This secoiridoid phenolic compound has been shown to possess antifungal properties, helping to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens and alleviate symptoms of fungal skin infections.
Vitamin E (Tocopherol): As an antioxidant, vitamin E helps protect the skin from oxidative stress caused by fungal infections, promoting wound healing and reducing inflammation.
Squalene: This triterpene has been found to exhibit antifungal activity, disrupting the cell membrane of fungal pathogens and preventing the spread of infection.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Olea europaea leaves are used due to their antifungal and antimicrobial properties, which help combat fungal infections.
Fruits: Olea europaea fruits, also known as olives, contain compounds that exhibit antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, making them suitable for treating fungal skin infections.
Barks: Olea europaea barks are used due to their rich antioxidant and antifungal content, which helps in soothing and treating fungal skin infections.
remedy_recipe: Combine 100 grams of cold-pressed extra virgin olive oil with 50 grams of beeswax in a small saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat while gently stirring with a spatula for 10 minutes.
Add 20 grams of dried olea europaea leaves and 10 grams of dried olea europaea flowers to the mixture.
Continue heating the mixture for another 10 minutes while stirring occasionally with a spatula.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container to create the final cream.

remedy_properties: Antifungal activity: Neem cream's antifungal property helps to inhibit the growth and reproduction of fungi, thus preventing the infection from spreading and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory action: Neem's anti-inflammatory property reduces redness, swelling, and itching associated with fungal skin infections, making it more comfortable for patients to manage their condition.

Antimicrobial synergy: Neem cream's combination of antifungal and antibacterial properties creates a synergistic effect that enhances the treatment outcome by targeting multiple microorganisms simultaneously.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Azadirachta indica, also known as neem, creams helps with fungal skin infection because of its potent antifungal properties. Neem has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat a variety of skin issues, including fungal infections. The active compounds present in neem, such as nimbin and nimandial, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, thereby reducing the severity of the infection. Additionally, neem creams have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the affected skin.
remedy_constituents: Azadirachtin: This triterpenoid acts as an antifungal agent by inhibiting the growth and development of fungal cells, thereby preventing the spread of fungal infections.
Melanin: The melanin present in Azadirachta indica has antioxidant and antifungal properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens on the skin.
Quercetin: This flavonoid acts as an antifungal and anti-inflammatory agent by inhibiting the production of inflammatory mediators and preventing the adhesion of fungal cells to the skin surface.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Their oil, also known as neem oil, is commonly used due to its antifungal and antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: They are used for their ability to combat fungal infections, thanks to their high concentration of neem's active compounds.
Barks: The bark of Azadirachta indica contains compounds that have antifungal and antibacterial properties, making it effective in treating fungal skin infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of azadirachta indica leaves and flowers in the early morning when they are at their highest potency.
Dry the harvested leaves and flowers in a warm place for 3-5 days to prevent microbial growth.
Grind the dried leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder.
Mix 20g of the powder with 100g of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and store the resulting azadirachta indica cream in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedy_properties: Inhibition of fungal growth: The cream's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and polyacetylenes, can inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens like Candida albicans, thereby preventing the infection from spreading.

Anti-inflammatory activity: Kansas coneflower contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with fungal skin infections, promoting a more comfortable healing process.

Antioxidant properties: The cream's antioxidant properties can help to protect the skin from further damage caused by free radicals, which are produced during the infection process, and promote healthy tissue regeneration.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia, also known as Kansas coneflower, creams helps with fungal skin infection because of its rich antifungal and antibacterial properties. The herb is known to inhibit the growth of fungi and bacteria, which causes the infection. The cream's active compounds, such as echinacoside and alkylamides, work together to soothe and calm the affected skin, promoting a healthy environment for the skin to recover. This natural remedy offers an effective alternative for treating fungal skin infections, providing relief and promoting skin healing.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have been shown to exhibit antifungal activity, which can help combat fungal skin infections by inhibiting the growth and proliferation of fungal pathogens.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as caffeic acid and ferulic acid, possess antioxidant and antifungal properties that can help reduce oxidative stress and fungal growth on the skin, ultimately alleviating fungal skin infections.
Alkylamides: These compounds have been found to exhibit antifungal activity by disrupting the cell membrane of fungal pathogens, thereby inhibiting their growth and proliferation on the skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain echinacoside, a compound that exhibits antimicrobial properties, helping to combat fungal infections.
Rhyzomes: They have a higher concentration of alkylamides, which have been shown to have antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating skin infections.
Leaves: They contain various flavonoids and phenolic acids with potential antifungal and antioxidant properties, contributing to the effectiveness of creams for fungal skin infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea angustifolia roots in late summer or early fall when roots are fully matured.
Dry the harvested echinacea roots in a single layer for 1-2 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Steep 1 cup of dried echinacea roots in 2 cups of boiling water for 5-10 minutes to create a strong infusion.
Strain the echinacea infusion and mix it with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil to create a base.
Whip the base mixture until it becomes smooth and creamy then pour it into a glass jar for storage.

remedy_properties: Antifungal compounds: Pot marigold contains antifungal compounds such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which inhibit the growth of fungi and reduce infection.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with fungal infections.
Antioxidant activity: Pot marigold's antioxidant compounds neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and exacerbate fungal infections, promoting healthy skin regeneration.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with fungal skin infection because of its potent antifungal and antimicrobial properties. The active compounds in Calendula, such as triterpenoids and carotenoids, exhibit strong inhibitory effects against fungi like Candida and dermatophytes. These compounds also promote wound healing and reduce inflammation, creating an environment that is less conducive to fungal growth. This makes Calendula creams an effective topical treatment for fungal skin infections, promoting a rapid recovery and alleviating symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds have been found to exhibit antifungal activity by inhibiting the growth of fungi and preventing them from penetrating the skin.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in Calendula officinalis have been shown to possess antimicrobial properties, which help in fighting fungal infections by preventing the spread of fungi and promoting wound healing.

Nordihydroguaiaretic acid (NDGA): NDGA is a phenolic compound that has been found to exhibit antifungal activity by interfering with the cell membrane of fungi, ultimately leading to their death and prevention of fungal skin infections.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, including triterpenoids and carotenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

Leaves: They are used in combination with flowers to provide a more holistic approach to treating fungal skin infections, as they contain a different set of bioactive compounds that complement the effects of the flowers.

Seeds: They are sometimes used in addition to flowers and leaves to enhance the effectiveness of the cream, as they contain a unique set of bioactive compounds that may have synergistic effects with the other plant parts.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 10 grams of dried calendula flowers in 200 ml of carrier oil like sweet almond oil for 2 hours at room temperature.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container to discard solids.
Combine 20 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler to melt the beeswax.
Mix 10 grams of the infused oil with 10 grams of shea butter and 20 grams of melted beeswax mixture.
Pour the final mixture into glass jars and let it cool and solidify completely at room temperature.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Geranium essential oil has antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of fungi, bacteria, and viruses, helping to combat fungal skin infections such as athlete's foot and ringworm.
Antifungal activity: The cream's antifungal properties help to reduce the severity of fungal infections by inhibiting the production of toxins and preventing further infection.

Anti-inflammatory activity: Geranium essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness, itching, and inflammation associated with fungal skin infections.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pelargonium graveolens, also known as geranium, creams helps with fungal skin infection because of its antifungal properties. The active compounds in this plant, such as geraniol and linalool, possess potent antifungal activity that targets and inhibits the growth of fungal pathogens. These compounds work by disrupting the cell membrane of fungal cells, ultimately leading to their death. This action helps in reducing the severity of fungal skin infections, including conditions like athlete's foot and ringworm, and promoting a healthy balance of skin microflora.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Linalool, a terpene, helps with fungal skin infections by exhibiting antifungal properties that inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens, such as Candida albicans.
Geraniol: Geraniol, a terpene, helps with fungal skin infections by displaying antimicrobial activity, which reduces the fungal load and prevents further colonization.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, a phenolic compound, helps with fungal skin infections by exhibiting antioxidant and antifungal properties, which protect the skin from oxidative stress and inhibit fungal growth.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of geraniol, which has antifungal and antimicrobial properties.
Flowers: Utilized for their essential oils, which contain geraniol and linalool, compounds with antifungal and antiseptic properties.
Stems: Employed for their leaves' secondary growth and for the essential oils they produce, which have similar antifungal properties as the leaves and flowers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried pelargonium graveolens leaves and flowers at their peak potency for optimal aroma release.
Steep the dried herb in 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at 60-70°c for 2 weeks.
Strain the oil mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container to separate the solids from the liquid.
Add 50g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter to the infused oil mixture and heat it gently until the waxes melt.
Pour the warm mixture into tin containers and allow it to cool and solidify before use as a topical cream.

remedy_properties: Antifungal compounds: Thyme contains antifungal compounds like carvacrol and thymol, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi such as Candida and Malassezia, helping to treat fungal skin infections like athlete's foot and ringworm.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with fungal skin infections, making it an effective treatment for conditions like eczema and dermatitis.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in thyme have been shown to combat oxidative stress and inflammation caused by fungal infections, promoting healing and reducing the risk of further infection.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, creams helps with fungal skin infection because of its potent antifungal properties. The active compounds in thyme, such as carvacrol and thymol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, reducing the severity of the infection. Thyme creams also promote a healthy skin environment, reducing inflammation and promoting the body's natural healing process. This makes thyme a popular natural remedy for fungal skin infections, providing relief and promoting recovery. Its antifungal properties make it an effective solution.
remedy_constituents: Carvacrol: Carvacrol has antifungal properties that inhibit the growth of fungi by disrupting cell membrane integrity, thereby preventing fungal infections.

Thymol: Thymol has antimicrobial and antifungal properties that help reduce fungal load and prevent infection by inhibiting the synthesis of ergosterol, an essential component of fungal cell membranes.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid has antioxidant and antifungal properties that help reduce oxidative stress and prevent fungal growth by inhibiting the production of fungal toxins and interfering with fungal cell membrane function.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of thymol, a natural antifungal compound used to treat fungal infections.
Buds: Containing essential oils, buds help to create a protective barrier against fungal growth and promote skin healing.
Seeds: Rich in thymol and other bioactive compounds, seeds are used to develop creams that combat fungal infections effectively.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves by cutting them just above the node using scissors.
Steam distill the leaves to extract the essential oil for 2 hours using a steam distillation apparatus.
Mix 100g of beeswax and 100g of shea butter in a double boiler to melt.
Combine the essential oil with 100ml of distilled water and 50ml of aloe vera gel in a bowl.
Blend the essential oil mixture with the melted beeswax mixture to create a uniform cream texture.

remedy_properties: Antifungal compounds: English lavender contains antifungal compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which inhibit the growth of fungi and prevent them from multiplying, thereby helping to clear up fungal skin infections.

Antibacterial properties: Lavender's antibacterial properties help to reduce bacterial colonization on the skin, creating an environment that is less conducive to fungal growth and making it easier for the body to heal itself.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lavender cream can help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness, itching, and swelling associated with fungal infections like athlete's foot or ringworm.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with fungal skin infection because of its antifungal and antibacterial properties. The active compounds present in lavender, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, exhibit potent antifungal activity against a range of fungi, including those that cause athlete's foot and ringworm. Additionally, lavender's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with fungal skin infections, promoting a faster recovery and soothing relief. This natural remedy offers a safe and effective alternative to conventional treatments.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: It acts as an antifungal agent, inhibiting the growth of fungi such as Trichophyton and Candida, which are common causes of fungal skin infections.
Lavandulol: This terpene has antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce the severity of fungal infections by inhibiting the development of fungal cells.
Caffeic acid: It exhibits antifungal activity by disrupting the cell membrane of fungal cells, ultimately preventing the spread of fungal infections.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They contain essential oils with antimicrobial properties that help combat fungal infections.
Leaves: They contain antioxidants and antimicrobial compounds that aid in soothing and healing fungal-infected skin.
Stems: They contain flavonoids and other compounds with antifungal properties that help reduce fungal growth and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers for use in cream production.
Combine 1 cup of distilled water with 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers to the double boiler mixture.
Remove from heat and let infuse for 2 hours then strain the mixture through a cheesecloth.
Whip 1/2 cup of shea butter with 1/4 cup of coconut oil to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Sage contains compounds like thymol and carnosic acid that have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, including Candida albicans, thereby helping to treat fungal skin infections.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage has anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid and ursolic acid that help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with fungal skin infections, promoting a faster recovery.

Antioxidant activity: Sage's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate fungal skin infections, while also promoting healthy wound healing and tissue repair.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, creams helps with fungal skin infection because of its antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The active compounds in sage, such as tannins and phenolic acids, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi and bacteria that cause skin infections. Additionally, sage's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation. Its antifungal properties help to combat fungal infections, such as athlete's foot and ringworm, by reducing the fungal load and promoting a healthy skin environment.
remedy_constituents: Ursolic acid: A triterpenoid with potent antimicrobial properties, including antifungal activity, which helps inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens on the skin.
Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound with strong antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, including antifungal effects, which can help reduce fungal growth and inflammation on the skin.
Salvianolic acid A: A phenolic compound with antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, including antifungal activity, which may help prevent the spread of fungal infections on the skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Salvia officinalis are rich in antioxidants and possess antimicrobial properties, making them effective in combating fungal infections.
Stems: The stems of Salvia officinalis contain rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to exhibit antifungal and antibacterial activities, helping to treat fungal skin infections.

Barks: The bark of Salvia officinalis contains compounds like tannins and flavonoids, which have been found to possess antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful in treating fungal skin infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated and before the heat of the day sets in.
Steep 30 grams of fresh salvia officinalis leaves in 1 liter of distilled water at 80°c for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid mixture through cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container to remove solids.
Combine the infused liquid with 200 grams of distilled water and 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler setup.
Stir the mixture continuously until the beeswax has fully dissolved and the mixture has cooled to a temperature suitable for pouring into containers.

remedy_properties: Antifungal compounds: Herbal ginger creams contain antifungal compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi such as Candida albicans, reducing the severity of fungal skin infections.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, including gingerols and shogaols, help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with fungal skin infections, promoting faster healing and relief from discomfort.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in herbal ginger creams, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of fungal infections.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with fungal skin infection because of its antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, including those that cause infections like athlete's foot and ringworm. Additionally, ginger's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with fungal skin infections, promoting faster healing and relief from discomfort. This makes ginger creams a promising natural remedy for treating fungal skin infections.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols are a group of phenolic compounds that exhibit antifungal properties by inhibiting the growth of fungi, thereby reducing the severity of fungal skin infections.
Shogaols: Shogaols, similar to gingerols, possess antifungal properties that help combat fungal skin infections by disrupting the fungal cell membrane and preventing the production of fungal toxins.

Zingiberene: Zingiberene, a sesquiterpene, has been shown to have antifungal activity by reducing the expression of genes involved in fungal cell wall synthesis, ultimately inhibiting the growth of fungi and alleviating fungal skin infections.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The most used part of Zingiber officinale in creams for fungal skin infections is the Rhyzomes, as they contain high concentrations of gingerols and shogaols, which have antifungal properties.
Roots: The Roots of Zingiber officinale are also used in creams for fungal skin infections due to their ability to inhibit the growth of fungi and promote healing of infected skin.
Barks: The Barks of Zingiber officinale are sometimes used in creams for fungal skin infections, as they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit antifungal and antimicrobial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried zingiber officinale root and grind it into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine 20g of the powdered root with 10g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes while stirring occasionally to ensure even melting.
Remove the double boiler from the heat and let the mixture cool to around 35°c before whipping it.
Whip the mixture for 5 minutes using a hand mixer until it thickens into a smooth cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon creams helps with fungal skin infection because of its potent antifungal properties. The unique compounds present in Ceylon cinnamon, such as cinnamaldehyde and cinnamic acid, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, including candida and other species that cause infections. These antifungal properties help to reduce inflammation, soothe itching and burning sensations, and promote healing. By applying a Ceylon cinnamon cream topically, individuals can effectively combat fungal skin infections and restore healthy, clear skin.
remedy_properties: Antifungal compounds: The cream contains antifungal compounds such as cinnamic acid, which inhibit the growth and reproduction of fungi, helping to reduce the severity of fungal skin infections.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory properties of Ceylon cinnamon creams can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with fungal skin infections, promoting a faster healing process.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant activity of Ceylon cinnamon creams helps protect the skin from further damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate fungal infections and impede the body's natural healing response.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that inhibit fungal growth and promote anti-inflammatory responses.
Barks: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making them effective against fungal skin infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality cinnamomum verum bark and dry it properly in the shade for two weeks.
Grind the dried cinnamomum verum bark into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix the powdered cinnamomum verum with 20 grams of coconut oil and 10 grams of beeswax in a double boiler for 15 minutes.
Heat the mixture until it reaches 60°c, then remove from heat and let it cool to 40°c for 30 minutes.
Pour the cooled mixture into sterilized jars and store them in the refrigerator at a temperature below 8°c for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic creams helps with fungal skin infection because of its antifungal and antibacterial properties, which work synergistically to combat the fungal growth. The sulfur compounds in garlic cream, such as allicin, disrupt the fungal cell membrane, inhibiting its ability to reproduce and spread. Additionally, garlic's antimicrobial properties help to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making it an effective natural remedy for treating fungal skin infections like athlete's foot, ringworm, and yeast infections.
remedy_properties: Antifungal compounds: Garlic cream contains sulfur compounds like allicin, which have been shown to exhibit antifungal activity against Candida albicans and other fungal species, inhibiting their growth and reducing infection.

Antioxidant activity: Garlic cream's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by fungal infections, promoting a healthy skin environment that is less conducive to fungal growth.

Immune system modulation: Herbal garlic creams may also contain immunomodulatory compounds that help modulate the immune response, enhancing the body's natural defenses against fungal infections and reducing the risk of infection recurrence.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the garlic plant contain a high concentration of allicin, which has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help to combat fungal infections.
Buds: The buds of the garlic plant are rich in sulfur compounds, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against fungi and bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Extract 200g of fresh garlic cloves by peeling and crushing them into a fine paste using mortar and pestle.
Combine the extracted garlic paste with 20ml of olive oil and 10ml of coconut oil in a heatproof glass bowl.
Heat the mixture at low temperature for 30 minutes or until the oils turn golden brown and fragrant, stirring occasionally.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into another container, squeezing gently to extract as much liquid as possible, discarding solids.
Mix the resulting infusion with 10g of beeswax and 20ml of sweet almond oil in a double boiler, heating until melted and well combined.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum creams helps with fungal skin infection because of its unique properties that provide a natural antifungal defense. The cream's active compounds, such as terpinen-4-ol and alpha-pinene, possess potent antifungal activities that effectively combat fungal growth and inhibit the reproduction of fungi. Additionally, the cream's soothing and moisturizing effects help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy skin cells, making it an effective treatment option for fungal infections such as athlete's foot, ringworm, and nail fungus.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum contains a compound called cineole, which has been shown to exhibit antifungal activity against a wide range of fungal species, including Candida and Aspergillus, making it effective in combating fungal skin infections.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The creams' anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with fungal skin infections, providing relief and promoting healing.

Antibacterial properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum's antibacterial activity helps prevent bacterial overgrowth, which can occur when the skin is compromised by a fungal infection, reducing the risk of secondary infections and promoting wound healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Tasmanian Blue Gum plant are used due to their antifungal and antimicrobial properties, which can help combat fungal infections on the skin.
Barks: The barks of the plant are rich in terpenoids, which have been shown to exhibit antifungal activity against various fungi that cause skin infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves from reputable suppliers or your own garden and grind into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground eucalyptus leaves with 200ml of coconut oil, 100ml of beeswax, and 20ml of jojoba oil in a double boiler at 65°c for 30 minutes.
Add 10ml of tea tree essential oil and 5ml of lavender essential oil to the mixture and stir well, ensuring even distribution of oils.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool slightly before pouring into glass jars, allowing the beeswax to solidify completely within 2 hours.
Once set, label and date the creams, and store them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or refrigerate for up to a year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Patchouli creams helps with fungal skin infection because of its antifungal properties, which are derived from the essential oils present in the patchouli plant. The cream's active compounds, such as patchoulol and α-bulnesene, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, including Candida and dermatophytes, that cause infections like athlete's foot and ringworm. By applying the cream directly to the affected area, it helps to reduce fungal colonization and alleviate symptoms like itching, redness, and scaling, promoting a faster recovery from infection.
remedy_properties: Antifungal activity: Patchouli oil has been shown to exhibit antifungal properties, which helps to combat fungal infections such as athlete's foot and ringworm by inhibiting the growth of fungi.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in patchouli oil help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with fungal skin infections, making it an effective ingredient for soothing irritated skin.
Antimicrobial properties: Patchouli oil's antimicrobial properties help to prevent the spread of infection by killing bacteria and other microorganisms that can exacerbate fungal infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of antibacterial and antifungal compounds, which help combat fungal infections.
Barks: The barks contain tannins, which have astringent properties that can help reduce inflammation and soothe skin irritations caused by fungal infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh pogostemon cablin leaves in a clean container and wash them with distilled water.
Dry the leaves using shade or low-temperature drying methods to retain their essential oils.
Grind dried pogostemon cablin leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 20 grams of powdered pogostemon cablin with 100 milliliters of coconut oil and heat mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and transfer to an amber glass bottle for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass creams helps with fungal skin infection because of its potent antifungal properties. The citrus oil present in lemongrass has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungus, reducing inflammation and itching associated with infections such as athlete's foot, ringworm, and toenail fungus. Additionally, lemongrass cream's anti-inflammatory compounds help to soothe and calm the skin, promoting a healthy environment for skin regeneration. Its natural antibacterial properties also prevent further infection, making it an effective remedy for fungal skin infections.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lemongrass contains compounds like citral, which have potent antimicrobial activity against fungi, bacteria, and viruses, helping to reduce the growth and spread of fungal infections on the skin.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemongrass has anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with fungal skin infections, promoting a more comfortable healing process.

Antifungal properties: The essential oils in lemongrass, such as citral and geraniol, have been shown to exhibit antifungal activity against various types of fungi, including those that cause skin infections like ringworm and athlete's foot.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that exhibit antifungal and antibacterial properties, making them effective against fungal skin infections.
Rhyzomes: Containing a high concentration of citral, a natural fungicide that helps combat fungal growth and promotes healing.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality cymbopogon citratus leaves and dry them completely using shade drying method for at least three days.
Grind dried cymbopogon citratus leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle, yielding approximately 200g of powdered herb.
Combine the ground herbal powder with 100g of coconut oil in a heatproof glass bowl, stirring until well mixed and uniform.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, allowing the herbal extract to infuse into the coconut oil, while occasionally stirring to prevent scorching.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, discarding solids and transferring the cream-like mixture to sterilized glass containers for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sandalwood creams helps with fungal skin infection because of its potent antifungal properties, which effectively combat fungal growth and eliminate existing infections. The cream's natural ingredients, such as terpenes and flavonoids, create an environment that is hostile to fungal development, promoting healthy skin and preventing further infection. Additionally, sandalwood's anti-inflammatory properties help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing discomfort and promoting a faster healing process.
remedy_properties: Antifungal activity: Sandalwood oil has been shown to inhibit the growth of various fungal species, including Candida albicans, Aspergillus niger, and Trichophyton rubrum, helping to combat fungal overgrowth on the skin.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The beta-sitosterol present in sandalwood cream reduces inflammation, which can exacerbate fungal skin infections by creating a favorable environment for fungal growth and impeding the healing process.

Antioxidant activity: Sandalwood cream's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from oxidative stress, which can contribute to fungal infection and skin damage. Antioxidants also promote healthy skin cell function, supporting the body's natural defenses against infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and antimicrobial properties, making them effective against fungal infections.
Barks: Containing a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which exhibit antifungal and antibacterial activities.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of dried santalum album leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered santalum album with 100 grams of coconut oil and 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler, heating for 30 minutes to melt the waxes.
Add 20 drops of tea tree essential oil and stir well, allowing the mixture to cool and thicken for 1 hour.
Pour the cooled mixture into sterilized cream jars and seal tightly, storing in a cool dark place for 2 weeks to allow for infusion and maturation.
Shake the jars gently every day during the 2-week period and then use the finished creams as needed, applying topically to affected areas twice daily for 6 weeks or until symptoms clear up.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary creams helps with fungal skin infection because of its potent antifungal properties. The essential oils present in rosemary, such as camphor and borneol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal organisms, including candida, yeast, and mold. Additionally, rosemary's antimicrobial properties help to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making it an effective natural treatment for skin infections like athlete's foot, ringworm, and toenail fungus. By applying rosemary creams directly to affected areas, individuals can combat fungal infections and promote healthy, clear skin.
remedy_properties: Antifungal compounds: Rosemary contains antifungal compounds like camphor, borneol, and bornyl acetate, which inhibit the growth of fungi and combat infections.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with fungal skin infections, making it an effective topical treatment for soothing affected areas.
Antioxidant activity: Rosemary's antioxidant properties can help protect the skin from oxidative stress caused by fungal infections, promoting a faster recovery and reducing the risk of scarring.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots have antimicrobial properties that help combat fungal infections and promote wound healing, making them an effective ingredient in creams.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome's anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties make it a valuable addition to creams, helping to reduce redness and itching caused by fungal skin infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves and dry them in shade for two weeks or until brittle.
Grind dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves into fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Mix 20 grams of the ground rosmarinus officinalis powder with 100 milliliters of distilled water to form paste.
Heat the paste over low heat for 10 minutes or until it reaches a temperature of 60 degrees celsius.
Combine the heated paste with 200 milliliters of beeswax and 50 grams of sweet almond oil, stirring until mixture cools and thickens.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with fungal skin infection because of its potent antifungal properties. The menthol content in peppermint has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, including candida and ringworm, which cause common fungal skin infections such as athlete's foot and nail fungus. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and itching associated with fungal infections. As a natural treatment option, herbal peppermint creams provide an effective and gentle way to combat fungal skin infections.
remedy_properties: Antifungal activity: Peppermint's active compounds, such as menthol and menthone, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi like Candida albicans, reducing their ability to cause infection.
Anti-inflammatory effects: The menthol in peppermint cream can help reduce inflammation associated with fungal skin infections, which can alleviate discomfort and promote healing.
Antioxidant properties: Peppermint's antioxidant compounds can help neutralize free radicals that may contribute to fungal growth and damage to the skin, promoting a healthy environment for recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Peppermint roots have anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, making them effective in reducing redness and swelling associated with fungal skin infections.
Barks: Peppermint barks contain salicylic acid, which helps to exfoliate the skin and promote healing by removing dead skin cells and fungi.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves, rinsing them thoroughly under cold running water then pat drying with paper towels.
Combine 20 grams of dried peppermint leaves with 100 milliliters of distilled water in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes at room temperature, allowing the menthol and menthone to infuse into the water.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bowl, discarding the solids then add 20 grams of beeswax.
Heat the infused liquid and beeswax mixture in a double boiler for 10 minutes stirring occasionally until the beeswax is fully melted.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for fungal skin infection are topical preparations made from natural herbs that help alleviate fungal skin infections by providing antifungal and soothing properties. The best herbal creams for fungal skin infection are Calendula officinalis, Aloe vera, Melaleuca alternifolia, Eucalyptus globulus, Aloe barbadensis, Cymbopogon citratus, Zingiber officinale, Echinacea purpurea, Lavandula angustifolia, and Sanguinaria canadensis. If left untreated, fungal skin infections can cause discomfort, itching, and embarrassment, negatively impacting daily life, social interactions, and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to fungal skin infection that are treated with herbal creams are acne, eczema, and psoriasis. Herbal creams containing ingredients like tea tree oil, chamomile, and aloe vera can help soothe and calm these conditions. These creams may reduce inflammation, prevent bacterial growth, and promote healing, offering natural relief from symptoms such as redness, itching, and irritation.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with fungal skin infection is a blend of tea tree oil, aloe vera, and neem. Tea tree oil has antifungal properties that combat the infection, while aloe vera soothes and calms the skin. Neem, rich in antimicrobial agents, helps to reduce inflammation and promote healing. This synergistic combination can effectively reduce fungal symptoms and promote a healthy recovery. Regular application of this blend can lead to improved skin health and a reduced risk of fungal infection recurrence.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by The Australasian journal of dermatology, herbal creams containing tea tree oil can be beneficial for fungal skin infections such as tinea pedis. The study, which involved 104 patients, found that tea tree oil cream (10% w/w) reduced symptomatology as effectively as the commonly used antifungal medication tolnaftate 1% in terms of clinical improvement. However, tea tree oil cream was no more effective than a placebo in achieving a mycological cure, suggesting that while it may help alleviate symptoms, it may not be as effective as traditional antifungal treatments in completely clearing the infection.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams helps with fungal skin infection because of its antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The cream's active compounds, including triterpenoids and carotenoids, work to inhibit the growth of fungi, reducing symptoms such as itching, redness, and inflammation. Additionally, calendula's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm the skin, promoting a healthy environment that is less conducive to fungal growth. This natural remedy provides a non-invasive and effective solution for treating fungal skin infections, such as athlete's foot and ringworm.
remedy_constituents: Oleanolic acid: It exhibits antifungal properties, which help inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens that cause skin infections.
Nordihydroguaiaretic acid (NDGA): It has potent antifungal and anti-inflammatory effects, which help combat fungal infections and reduce associated inflammation and redness.
Quercetin: It possesses antifungal and antioxidant properties, which help prevent fungal pathogens from causing infections and reduce oxidative stress in the affected skin area.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are widely used due to their high concentration of triterpenoid saponins, which have antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Leaves contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids that contribute to their antifungal and antimicrobial properties, making them suitable for treating fungal skin infections.

Stems: Stems of Calendula officinalis contain triterpenoid saponins, which are known for their antifungal and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to soothe and protect the skin affected by fungal infections.
remedy_recipe: Infuse dried calendula flowers in a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or grapeseed oil for 2-3 weeks to create a herbal infusion.
Strain the herbal infusion through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the plant material and reserve the liquid.
Mix the herbal infusion with a thickening agent like beeswax or candelilla wax to achieve a desired consistency.
Blend the mixture with a moisturizing ingredient such as shea butter or coconut oil to enhance skin nourishment.
Pour the final mixture into a clean glass jar and store it in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams helps with fungal skin infection because of its natural anti-fungal and soothing properties. The gel of the Aloe vera plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which exhibit antifungal activity against various fungal pathogens. Additionally, Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with fungal skin infections. This makes Aloe vera creams an effective and natural remedy for treating fungal skin infections, promoting faster healing and soothing the skin.
remedy_constituents: Acemannan: Has antifungal properties that inhibit the growth and proliferation of fungal cells, thereby reducing the severity of fungal skin infections.
Aloin: Exhibits antifungal and antibacterial activities that help in preventing the spread of fungal infections and promoting a healthy skin environment.
Salicylic acid: Possesses antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties that help in reducing inflammation, soothing the skin, and preventing the recurrence of fungal skin infections.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The gel inside the leaves is used to make creams for fungal skin infections because of its soothing, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties.
Leaves: The extract from the leaves also contains compounds that help in reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
Leaves: The antioxidants present in the leaves help in reducing oxidative stress and promoting the recovery of fungal skin infections.
remedy_recipe: Combine 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel with 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a mixing bowl.
Gradually add 1 teaspoon of beeswax and 1/2 teaspoon of vitamin e oil to the mixture.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 5-7 minutes or until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool and thicken for about 30 minutes.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer until it becomes smooth and creamy in texture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with fungal skin infection because it contains tea tree oil, a natural antifungal agent. The oil's active compound, cineole, inhibits the growth of fungi and promotes a healthy balance of skin flora. The cream's antimicrobial properties help to reduce inflammation and prevent the spread of infection, promoting a faster healing process. Additionally, tea tree oil's antiseptic properties help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and discomfort associated with fungal skin infections.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Acts as an antimicrobial agent, helping to inhibit the growth and spread of fungal pathogens on the skin.
Linalool: Exhibits antifungal properties, helping to combat fungal infections by disrupting fungal cell membranes and inhibiting their metabolic processes.
Viridiflorol: Possesses antifungal and antimicrobial activities, helping to reduce fungal loads and promote healing in infected skin areas.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Melaleuca alternifolia are commonly used to make creams for fungal skin infections due to their high content of tea tree oil, which has antifungal properties.
Roots: The roots of Melaleuca alternifolia are used to make creams for fungal skin infections due to their ability to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
Barks: The barks of Melaleuca alternifolia are used to make creams for fungal skin infections due to their antimicrobial properties, which help combat fungal infections.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100 grams of melaleuca alternifolia leaves and dry them for 24 hours in a warm place.
Grind 20 grams of dried leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Combine 20 grams of melaleuca alternifolia powder with 100 grams of coconut oil and 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 15 minutes or until it reaches 180-190 degrees fahrenheit.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and pour it into small containers to cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams helps with fungal skin infection because it contains eucalyptol, a natural compound with antifungal and antibacterial properties. Eucalyptol helps to reduce inflammation and promote healing, creating an environment that is less conducive to fungal growth. Additionally, eucalyptus oil has been shown to exhibit antiseptic properties, helping to eliminate the fungal infection and prevent its spread. This makes eucalyptus globulus creams a popular natural remedy for fungal skin infections, such as athlete's foot and ringworm.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Helps to reduce inflammation and relieve symptoms associated with fungal skin infections, such as itching and redness.
Eugenol: Exhibits antifungal properties, inhibiting the growth of fungi that cause infections and promoting a healthy skin environment.
α-Terpineol: Demonstrates antimicrobial and antifungal activities, helping to combat fungal infections and reduce the risk of further infection.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Eucalyptus leaves are widely used due to their high content of eucalyptol, which has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help combat fungal skin infections.
Barks: The bark of Eucalyptus globulus contains compounds with antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating fungal skin infections and promoting wound healing.
Stems: Eucalyptus stems are another source of essential oils, including eucalyptol, which has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help prevent the spread of fungal infections.
remedy_recipe: Extract 2 ounces of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves in a saucepan with 4 cups of distilled water.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes or until the liquid has reduced to 1 cup.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl.
Combine 1/2 cup of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler.
Add the eucalyptus globulus extract to the cooled coconut oil mixture and whip until smooth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with fungal skin infection because of its anti-fungal and soothing properties. The gel extracted from the Aloe vera plant contains compounds like aloe-emodin, aloin, and lupeol, which exhibit antifungal activity against various fungal species. These compounds inhibit the growth and proliferation of fungal cells, reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Aloe vera's antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties also help to prevent infection and reduce discomfort associated with fungal skin infections, making it a natural and effective remedy for fungal skin infections.
remedy_constituents: Aloin: Helps by inhibiting the growth of fungal cells and preventing the formation of biofilms, thereby reducing the severity of fungal infections.
Acemannan: Assists by modulating the immune response and increasing the production of cytokines, which helps in combating fungal infections and promoting healing.
Cinnamaldehyde: Contributes by exhibiting antifungal properties, including the ability to disrupt the cell membrane of fungal cells and prevent the production of fungal toxins.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which help soothe and treat fungal skin infections.

Gel (from Leaves): More specifically, the clear gel found inside Aloe barbadensis leaves is commonly used as it has potent antifungal and antibacterial properties, making it effective against fungal skin infections.

Gel (from Leaves) is also often combined with other components from the leaves, such as Aloe Vera Extract (from Leaves): The extract from Aloe barbadensis leaves contains compounds that exhibit antifungal and antibacterial properties, contributing to its effectiveness in treating fungal skin infections.
remedy_recipe: Extract 250g of aloe barbadensis gel from 1 kg of fresh plant material and strain it through cheesecloth.
Combine 100g of aloe barbadensis gel with 100g of distilled water and 20g of glycerin in a stainless steel bowl.
Heat the mixture at 60c for 10 minutes in a double boiler setup then let it cool to 40c.
Add 10g of beeswax and 10g of stearic acid to the cooled mixture and heat it at 50c for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a silicone mold and let it set at room temperature for 30 minutes before removing the aloe barbadensis cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus creams helps with fungal skin infection because of its natural antifungal properties. The active compound, citral, has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, including Candida and Trichophyton, which are common causes of skin infections. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce redness, itching, and discomfort associated with fungal skin infections. Additionally, Cymbopogon citratus has been traditionally used to promote wound healing and prevent infection, making it a valuable natural remedy for treating fungal skin infections.
remedy_constituents: Citral: Exhibits antifungal properties by inhibiting the growth of fungal cells and preventing the spread of infections.
Geraniol: Displays antifungal and antibacterial activities, which help to combat fungal skin infections by reducing the growth of pathogens.
Citronellal: Possesses antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in soothing and healing fungal skin infections while preventing further infection.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help combat fungal skin infections.
Rhyzomes: Employed for their citral content, which exhibits antimicrobial and antifungal activities, aiding in the treatment of fungal infections.
Leaves: Also used due to their high oil content, which includes citral and geraniol, compounds known for their antifungal properties.
remedy_recipe: Extract 20 grams of dried cymbopogon citratus leaves in a glass container using 500 milliliters of carrier oil.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a separate glass container discarding the solids.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of shea butter with 50 milliliters of distilled water.
Combine the infused oil with the beeswax mixture and heat it until smooth and creamy consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with fungal skin infection because of its antifungal properties, derived from the natural compound gingerol. The active ingredient has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, such as Candida and dermatophytes, which cause infections like athlete's foot and ringworm. The cream's antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties also help to soothe and heal the affected skin, reducing redness and discomfort. By targeting the root cause of the infection, Zingiber officinale creams provide a natural and effective solution.
remedy_constituents: Shogaols: Shogaols have antifungal properties, which help to inhibit the growth and proliferation of fungal pathogens on the skin, thereby reducing the severity of fungal skin infections.
Gingerol: Gingerol possesses antimicrobial and antifungal activities, helping to combat fungal skin infections by suppressing the growth of fungal pathogens and promoting a healthy skin environment.

Curcuminoids: Curcuminoids exhibit potent antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and prevent the spread of fungal infections on the skin, promoting faster recovery and healing.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds with antimicrobial properties.
Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale contain compounds that help in reducing inflammation and promoting healing, making them a suitable choice for treating fungal skin infections.
Stems: The stems of the plant contain antibacterial and antifungal properties, which help in controlling the growth of fungi and bacteria that cause skin infections.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 20g of dried zingiber officinale in 200ml of carrier oil like coconut oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids for 10 minutes.
Combine 50ml of the infused oil with 20g of beeswax and heat in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Add 5ml of vitamin e oil and 5ml of preservative to the mixture and stir well for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify for 1 hour.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams helps with fungal skin infection because of its antifungal and antibacterial properties. The active compounds in Echinacea purpurea, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens, including Candida and Aspergillus. These compounds also exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with fungal skin infections. By promoting a healthy skin environment and inhibiting the growth of pathogens, Echinacea purpurea creams can aid in the treatment and prevention of fungal skin infections.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: They possess antifungal properties that can inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens, thereby reducing the severity of fungal skin infections.

Flavonoids (specifically Kaempferol and Quercetin): These compounds exhibit antifungal and antioxidant activities, which can help in combating fungal infections and reducing inflammation in the affected skin area.

Caffeic acid: It has been shown to exhibit antifungal activity against various fungal pathogens, including those causing skin infections, by inhibiting the fungal cell wall synthesis and metabolic processes.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in alkaloids, particularly echinulin, which has antifungal properties that can help combat fungal infections.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and phenolic acids with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can aid in soothing and treating fungal skin infections.
Flowers: They have antifungal properties due to the presence of alkylamides, which can help inhibit the growth of fungi and promote healing in affected skin areas.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea purpurea roots and flowers at dawn when they are fresh and full of moisture.
Dry the harvested echinacea purpurea roots and flowers in a warm oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit.
Combine 100 grams of dried echinacea purpurea roots and flowers with 100 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of coconut oil.
Stir the mixture continuously for 10 minutes and then remove it from the heat source.
Pour the cooled mixture into glass jars and store them in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams helps with fungal skin infection because of its antifungal properties, derived from the essential oils of the lavender plant. The active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, thereby reducing the severity of the infection. This natural remedy promotes a healthy balance of skin flora, accelerating the healing process and reducing inflammation, making it a popular choice for treating fungal skin conditions, including athlete's foot and ringworm.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: It exhibits antifungal properties by inhibiting the growth of fungal cells and preventing them from adhering to the skin, thereby reducing the severity of fungal skin infections.
Linalyl acetate: This terpene helps to prevent fungal growth and inhibit the production of fungal toxins, ultimately reducing the symptoms of fungal skin infections.
Caryophyllene oxide: It demonstrates antifungal and antibacterial activities, which help to reduce the risk of secondary infections and promote the healing of fungal skin lesions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain antifungal compounds that help combat fungal infections.
Flowers: Rich in antifungal and antiseptic properties, they aid in soothing and healing fungal-infected skin.
Seeds: Containing antifungal compounds, they contribute to the antifungal properties of creams for skin infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers at peak bloom to ensure optimal potency and fragrance.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer at 35°c for 7 days to preserve their essential oils.
Infuse the dried flowers in 200ml of cold-pressed jojoba oil at a ratio of 1:2 for 2 weeks to create a potent extract.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and mix it with 20g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter to create a smooth consistency.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and reaches a temperature of 40°c to create a soothing and fragrant cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sanguinaria canadensis creams helps with fungal skin infection because of its antifungal properties. The cream contains alkaloids, specifically sanguinarine, which is known to inhibit the growth of fungi. This natural compound helps to reduce inflammation and promote healing of the affected skin. The cream's antiseptic properties also help to prevent the spread of infection, promoting a healthy environment for the skin to recover. As a result, Sanguinaria canadensis creams offer a natural and effective solution for treating fungal skin infections.
remedy_constituents: Protopine: This alkaloid compound exhibits antifungal properties, helping to inhibit the growth and proliferation of fungal pathogens that cause skin infections.

Sanguinarine: A potent alkaloid with antifungal and antimicrobial activities, sanguinarine can help combat fungal skin infections by disrupting the cell membranes and metabolic processes of the fungal pathogens.

Oxysanguinarine: This alkaloid has been shown to possess antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fungal skin infections, such as redness, itching, and swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Sanguinaria canadensis contain berberine, a compound with antifungal properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Sanguinaria canadensis contain berberine and other alkaloids that exhibit antifungal and anti-inflammatory activities.
Roots: The roots of Sanguinaria canadensis also contain sanguinarine, another alkaloid with antifungal and antimicrobial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sanguinaria canadensis roots and leaves in the fall to ensure maximum potency and safety.
Dry the collected roots and leaves for 2 weeks at 35 degrees celsius to prevent mold formation.
Make a tincture using 1 part sanguinaria canadensis root and 2 parts glycerin in a ratio of 1:2.
Combine the tincture with 10 grams of beeswax and 5 milliliters of coconut oil to create a stable base.
Emulsify the mixture for 30 minutes to an hour using a hand mixer or a double boiler.
